production_targets_reviewed: schema/json fixtures loaded by authorization/evidence/governance/release/source schema tests
decision_reason: Same load/assertion shape, but each member validates a different schema or release artifact with different fixture semantics.

Shared copy_candidate setup and contains assertions, but each member mutates a different artifact field: temporary path, manifest finality, PR binding, extra files, baseline drift, or non-json artifact.
Common observation generator target, but members cover distinct observation semantics: stale/EOD, live spread, price direction, threshold proximity, volume, and failed observations.

production_targets_reviewed: frontend static/read_text contracts across accessibility, observability, readonly, M5N
decision_reason: Static text assertions overlap, but files guard different surfaces: accessibility labels, observability panels, readonly schema wording, and M5N workbench contract.
Shared datetime assertion shape, but each member validates a different source adapter timestamp/freshness transformation.
All read text and check tokens, but they protect separate docs/workflows: freshness matrix, PR checklist, pytest markers, repo-wide governance, and test segmentation.
Same builder target, but stale, delayed, and live-candidate caveats are distinct display semantics.
Shared helper/setup path, but members exercise distinct staging candidate failure and manifest behavior.
Same datetime target and equality shape, but members cover before, exact authorized, pre-expiry, exact expiry, and receipt audit boundaries.

production_targets_reviewed: source registry and adapter matrix validators
decision_reason: Common any/equality pattern, but assertions check separate registry and adapter error families.
Same adapter report target, but members cover official OpenAPI mapping, malformed summaries, failed targets, and integration shape.
Same renderer and contains assertions, but members cover raw collection suppression, market-session dict rendering, table escaping, and empty-list rendering.
Both check boolean flags from the same builder, but one validates readonly_only positive construction while the other validates multiple false governance flags.
Shared load/truthy shape, but files validate different production-safety metadata in token, evidence, and readiness artifacts.
Same Yahoo probe helper shape, but members cover separate identity mismatch and batch failure cases.
Common PR body parser target, but members cover exact match, extra nonexistent file, missing changed file warning, and duplicate entry warning.
Same environment helper shape, but members cover prohibited sources, max target enforcement, and source rejection.

production_targets_reviewed: fixture replay, audit ledger, risk flag, workflow policy JSON
decision_reason: Shared JSON load plus contains assertions, but each member validates a distinct policy/fixture schema.
Path token overlap only; members protect distinct workflow and convergence documentation contracts.
Same payload helper shape, but members cover different validator acceptance/rejection families.
Identical validate()==[] assertion shape across different validator modules and artifacts, so semantic equivalence is false.
Same pack builder and contains assertions, but members verify source baseline, usable live filtering, and prohibited vocabulary absence.
Frontend path/static overlap, but one guards M5K endpoint model while the other guards source-health panel contract.
Same request validator target, but one checks duplicate/noncanonical run dir and another checks binding/schema requirements.
Shared helper has no direct asserts in wrapper, but each member feeds distinct TWSE MIS freshness fixtures.
Same MIS parser target, but members validate price fallback, unavailable price, and numeric price preference.
Same summary builder, but one covers successful source summary and one covers None/failure summary.
Same datetime equality shape, but source-specific timestamp fields and timezone preferences differ.
Same schema checker call but distinct schema documents: authorization token, evidence ledger, and source contract.
Same load_valid helper, but members cover different M5A authorization fixture families.
Both write malformed files, but expected structured outcomes differ between request and result envelopes.

All eight members used the same VALID_CONTROLLED_ARGS, the same monkeypatch that raises on runner execution, the same MCP tool call, and the same four assertions; the preserved owner keeps the legacy controlled tool fail-closed/no-network/no-write guard.
